def create_charts(df):
    import pandas as pd
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
    
    # Aggregating data for the first chart
    medication_status = df.groupby(['Medication', 'Status']).agg({'Count': 'sum'}).reset_index()
    
    # Creating the first figure: Bar chart of medication counts by status
    fig1 = go.Figure()
    statuses = medication_status['Status'].unique()
    for status in statuses:
        status_data = medication_status[medication_status['Status'] == status]
        fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(
            x=status_data['Medication'],
            y=status_data['Count'],
            name=status
        ))
    fig1.update_layout(
        title='Medication Counts by Status',
        xaxis_title='Medication',
        yaxis_title='Count',
        barmode='stack',
        template='plotly_white'
    )
    
    # Aggregating data for the second chart
    medication_total = df.groupby('Medication').agg({'Count': 'sum'}).reset_index()
    
    # Creating the second figure: Pie chart of total counts by medication
    fig2 = go.Figure(
        go.Pie(
            labels=medication_total['Medication'],
            values=medication_total['Count'],
            hole=.3
        )
    )
    fig2.update_layout(
        title='Total Counts by Medication',
        template='plotly_white'
    )
    
    return fig1, fig2

    import pandas as pd
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
    
    # Aggregating data for the first chart

        status_data = medication_status[medication_status['Status'] == status]
        fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(
            x=status_data['Medication'],

    fig1.update_layout(

    # Aggregating data for the second chart
    medication_total = df.groupby('Medication').agg({'Count': 'sum'}).reset_index()
